session.user:
Run the following command and report results. Do NOT fix anything, just report:
``` cd /home/runner/work/erk/erk && uv run pytest tests/tui/ tests/erk_shared/gateway/plan_data_provider/ -x -q 2>&1 | tail -20 ```
Report whether all tests passed, the total count, or list any failures.
session.assistant: I'll run the test command and report the results.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Run pytest on tui and plan_data_provider tests

session.tool_result:
"""Key mappings should resolve to the correct command IDs.""" row = make_plan_row( 123, "Test Plan", plan_url="https://github.com/test/repo/issues/123", pr_number=456, ) ctx = CommandContext(row=row, view_mode=ViewMode.PLANS) screen = LaunchScreen(ctx=ctx)
assert screen._key_to_command_id["c"] == "close_plan" assert screen._key_to_command_id["d"] == "dispatch_to_queue" > assert screen._key_to_command_id["f"] == "rebase_remote" ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E KeyError: 'f'
tests/tui/screens/test_launch_screen.py:132: KeyError =========================== short test summary info ============================ FAILED tests/tui/screens/test_launch_screen.py::test_launch_screen_maps_command_ids_correctly - KeyError: 'f' !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! stopping after 1 failures !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
session.assistant:
**Test Results:**
pytest failed: 1 issue found
**Failure:**
``` FAILED tests/tui/screens/test_launch_screen.py::test_launch_screen_maps_command_ids_correctly - KeyError: 'f'
File: tests/tui/screens/test_launch_screen.py:132 Error: KeyError when accessing screen._key_to_command_id["f"] Expected: "rebase_remote" key mapping Actual: Key 'f' does not exist in the mapping ```
The test expects a key mapping for "f" → "rebase_remote", but that key is not present in `screen._key_to_command_id`. This indicates a mismatch between the test expectations and the actual implementation of LaunchScreen's command key mappings.
